rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF PETER ZIDAN KWAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/1/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) This hearing was 3/4/26, not 3/3/26. Notice must be given for the 4/1/26 hearing. (2) A reappraisal (for sale) does NOT change the carry value of assets. A subsequent accounting must always begin with the assets on hand in the prior accounting. (3) As with the accounting, the I&A values should be used for the basis of the statutory fee calculation, not reappraisal for sale values. In any event, the math in paragraph 4 of the supplement filed on 2/17/26 does not appear to be correct. Please provide a revised proposed order (if applicable) and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 3/23/26. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MAURICE ELLIS GOLDMAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/15/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (2) When an accounting is presented, original bank or brokerage statements must be filed to verify the ending balance(s) shown in the accounting. None appears to have been filed. [LRSF 14.34 D.] See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. (3) Cash in excess of $250,000 was reportedly kept in an interest bearing account, but the accounting does not reflect any interest. (4) A notice of remote appearance is not required to be filed in Probate proceedings. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 4/1/26. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. NB: In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate and distribute an estate must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution." [LRSF 14.35 I.6.b.]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ROBERT D. DOCKENDORFF 2005 REVOCABLE TRUST, AS AMENDED AND RESTATED
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Approval Of Settlement Agreement
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, although notice was defective (no instructions for remote appearance attached to the notice). The terms of the settlement agreement appear to be a modification of the now irrevocable trust, which requires the consent of all beneficiaries. [Probate Code Section 15403.] The Gay, Lesbian, Bisexual, Transgender Historical Society is the beneficiary of 60% of the net proceeds from the sale of the residence and the residue of the trust under the terms of the 2024 restated trust. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE CHISUM FAMILY TRUST DATED MARCH 1, 2001 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Instruction Re: Confirming Trustee Of Trust And Request To Ratify Actions Of Maura Albrecht As Attorney-In-Fact
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/15/2026. Issue necessitating continuance: still no proof of service of proposed order has been filed with the court. Any party lodging a proposed order in a contested matter must file proof of service of the proposed order on all parties entitled to notice of the petition. (San Francisco Local Rule 14.7.A.4). Please address the above issue and provide courtesy copies of all further filings and a proposed order to this examiner no later than 04/01/2026 to avoid additional continuance.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE IN THE MATTER OF THE ELAINE D CAHN TRUST DATED TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Suspension Of Powers And Removal Of Co-Trustees And Appointment Of Private Professional Fiduciary
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/24/2026 per stipulation of both counsel (via e-mail on 01/27/2026) to be calendared with the related petition and to be reviewed by the same examiner to whom the related petition was previously assigned. A preliminary review indicates that the following issues may need to be addressed: 1) The address of Jewish Family and Children's Services set forth in the petition is different from its address listed on the proof of service. 2) The proposed successor trustee's bond, if required by the Court (see Probate Code section 15602(a)(2)(3) & (b)), must be addressed. In addition to the total market value of the trust assets on hand, the probable annual gross income therefrom must be bonded. See LRSF 14.16(G).

Please address the above issues and deliver courtesy copies of all further filings to this examiner no later than 04/01/2026 to avoid further continuance.
